task_completion.description: Navigate to the Contacts module in the CRM.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The VLM should see the vTiger CRM interface with the 'Contacts' module active, indicated by the highlighted tab or page header.

task_completion.description: Enter the custom view name 'New York Contacts'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The VLM should see a 'Create View' or 'Custom View' form with the text 'New York Contacts' entered into the View Name input field.

task_completion.description: Select the required columns for the list view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The VLM should see the column selection area of the custom view form containing exactly or including: First Name, Last Name, Primary Email, Mobile Phone, and Mailing City.

task_completion.description: Apply a filter condition where Mailing City equals New York.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The VLM should see the filter/conditions section of the form with a rule set to: Column 'Mailing City', Operator 'equals' (or 'e'), and Value 'New York'.

task_completion.description: Save the custom view and display the filtered results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The VLM should see the final Contacts list view page with the active view dropdown showing 'New York Contacts', and a data table displaying the selected columns and filtered records.

integrity.description: The agent used the web GUI to create the view.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show a web browser interacting with the vTiger CRM interface, not a terminal window running curl commands, SQL scripts, or API calls.

integrity.description: The agent actually created the view in the application rather than editing HTML.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface should look like a standard vTiger CRM page with functional dropdowns, standard styling, and proper layout, without signs of browser developer tools being used to mock the text.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write the expected outcome in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots must show the actual CRM software, not a text editor (like Notepad or VSCode) containing the words 'New York Contacts'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected view name is exactly 'New York Contacts'. The filter must be applied on the 'Mailing City' column (internal API identifier: 'mailingcity'), with the comparator 'equals' (internal identifier: 'e'), and the value 'New York'. The view must include the columns First Name ('firstname'), Last Name ('lastname'), and Mailing City ('mailingcity'), along with Primary Email and Mobile Phone as requested in the prompt.
